title: INITIATION
character_focus: Meet Oustaz Abou Adam, your guide

vocabulary.example_sentence: عندما تقابل صديقك تقول: السلام عليكم
vocabulary.example_translation: When you meet your friend you say: Peace be upon you

vocabulary.english: And upon you be peace (Response to greeting)
vocabulary.example_translation: You reply to the greeting: And upon you be peace
In the morning you say: Good morning
In the evening you say: Good evening
When you ask your friend: How are you?
You reply: I am well, praise be to God
What is your name, my friend?

vocabulary.english: It pleases me / Nice to meet you
vocabulary.example_translation: It pleases me to meet you

vocabulary.example_sentence: هل تريد ماء؟ نعم، من فضلك
vocabulary.example_translation: Do you want water? Yes, please

vocabulary.example_sentence: هل أنت تعب؟ لا، أنا بخير
vocabulary.example_translation: Are you tired? No, I'm fine
I am a student at the university
He is an Arabic language teacher

grammar_topics.title: Personal Pronouns (أنا، أنت، هو، هي)
grammar_topics.description: In Arabic, personal pronouns are used to refer to people. The main pronouns are: أنا (I), أنت (you-male), أنتِ (you-female), هو (he), and هي (she).
grammar_topics.rule: Each pronoun has a specific form and is used based on the gender and number of the person being referred to.

examples.explanation: Using أنا (I) to refer to oneself
Using أنت (you-male) to address a male
Using هو (he) to refer to a male person
Using هي (she) to refer to a female person

grammar_topics.title: Basic Verb Structure - Present Tense of 'to be'
grammar_topics.description: In Arabic, the verb 'to be' (يكون) is often implied rather than stated explicitly. When describing someone or something, we simply place the subject and description together.
grammar_topics.rule: Subject + Description = Complete sentence (verb 'to be' is implied)

grammar_topics.title: Islamic Greetings and Responses
grammar_topics.description: Arabic has specific greetings that carry cultural and religious significance. The most common is 'السلام عليكم' (peace be upon you) and the response 'وعليكم السلام' (and upon you be peace).
grammar_topics.rule: Greetings follow a pattern: greeting + response. Other greetings include صباح الخير (good morning) and مساء الخير (good evening).

examples.english: Peace be upon you - And upon you be peace
examples.explanation: The primary Islamic greeting and its response

examples.english: How are you? - I am well

dialogues.title: First Meeting
dialogues.situation: Two people meet for the first time in Medina

script.speaker: Ahmed
script.english: Peace be upon you

script.speaker: Fatima
script.english: And upon you be peace and God's mercy
script.phonetic: wa alaykum assalam wa rahmat allah

script.speaker: Ahmed
script.english: My name is Ahmed

script.speaker: Fatima
script.english: It pleases me to meet you. I am Fatima

script.speaker: Ahmed
script.english: Where are you from, Fatima?

script.speaker: Fatima
script.arabic: أنا من مصر، والحمد لله على السلامة
script.english: I am from Egypt, praise be to God for your safe arrival
script.phonetic: ana min misr, wa alhamd lillah ala assalam

dialogues.title: At the Coffee Shop
dialogues.situation: A student orders tea at a local café

script.speaker: Waiter
script.english: Welcome, peace be upon you

script.speaker: Student
script.arabic: وعليكم السلام. أريد شاي من فضلك
script.english: And upon you be peace. I want tea, please
script.phonetic: wa alaykum assalam. ureed shai min fadlak

script.speaker: Waiter
script.arabic: نعم، شاي بسكر أم بدون سكر؟
script.english: Yes, tea with sugar or without sugar?
script.phonetic: na'am, shai bi sukkar am bidun sukkar

script.speaker: Student
script.english: With sugar, please

script.speaker: Waiter
script.english: Right away. Do you also want bread?

script.speaker: Student
script.english: Yes, thank you

dialogues.title: Classroom Introduction
dialogues.situation: Teacher introduces himself to new students

script.speaker: Teacher
script.english: Peace be upon you, students

script.speaker: Class
script.english: And upon you be peace, teacher

script.speaker: Teacher
script.arabic: أنا معلم اللغة العربية. اسمي هو الدكتور سليم
script.english: I am an Arabic language teacher. My name is Dr. Salim
script.phonetic: ana mu'allim alugha alarabiya, ismi huwa addoktor salim

script.speaker: Class
script.english: Welcome, doctor

script.speaker: Teacher
script.english: Thank you. We will learn Arabic together

dialogues.title: Asking for Help
dialogues.situation: A student asks a friend for help

script.speaker: Student 1
script.english: Peace be upon you, my friend

script.speaker: Student 2
script.english: And upon you be peace. How are you?

script.speaker: Student 1
script.arabic: أنا بخير. هل تستطيع أن تساعدني؟
script.english: I am well. Can you help me?
script.phonetic: ana bikhair, hal tastatee an tusaadny

script.speaker: Student 2
script.english: Yes, please. In what?

script.speaker: Student 1
script.arabic: أحتاج إلى مساعدة في اللغة العربية
script.english: I need help with Arabic language
script.phonetic: ahtaj ila musaada fi alugha alarabiya

dialogues.title: Daily Farewell
dialogues.situation: Two friends say goodbye at the end of the day

script.speaker: Zainab
script.arabic: يا نور، أنا ذاهبة إلى البيت الآن
script.english: Noor, I am going home now
script.phonetic: ya noor, ana zahiba ila albayt al'an

script.speaker: Noor
script.english: Okay. See you later, Zainab

script.speaker: Zainab
script.english: Goodbye. Good night

script.speaker: Noor
script.english: And you too be well

cultural_insights.title: Islamic Greetings - The Heart of Arabic Culture
cultural_insights.description: The greeting 'السلام عليكم' (peace be upon you) is more than just a hello. It carries spiritual and cultural significance. It's a prayer for the well-being of the person you're addressing. This greeting is used by Muslims worldwide and reflects the values of peace and blessing in Islamic culture.
cultural_insights.relevance: Understanding these greetings helps you connect authentically with Arabic speakers and shows respect for their culture.

cultural_insights.title: Hospitality in Arab Culture
cultural_insights.description: Arabs place tremendous value on hospitality. The phrase 'أهلاً وسهلاً' (Ahlan wa Sahlan - literally 'your family and ease') is not just a welcome, but an invitation into the family. Offering tea, coffee, or refreshments to guests is a fundamental part of Arab hospitality.
cultural_insights.relevance: When you learn to greet and respond appropriately, you're participating in this beautiful tradition of hospitality.

cultural_insights.title: The Importance of Respect in Address
cultural_insights.description: Arabic has different forms of address based on gender, age, and social status. Using the correct form shows respect and understanding. For example, 'يا معلم' (O teacher) or 'يا دكتور' (O doctor) is used to show respect to professionals and elders.
cultural_insights.relevance: Learning these respectful forms of address is essential for proper communication in Arab society.

exercises.question: What does 'السلام عليكم' mean?
exercises.explanation: Correct! 'السلام عليكم' is the primary Islamic greeting meaning 'Peace be upon you'.

exercises.question: How do you respond to 'السلام عليكم'?
exercises.explanation: Correct! The proper response is 'وعليكم السلام' (And upon you be peace).

exercises.question: How do you say 'My name is Muhammad'?
exercises.explanation: Correct! 'اسمي محمد' means 'My name is Muhammad'.

exercises.question: What does 'بخير' mean?
exercises.explanation: Correct! 'بخير' means well or good, used to respond to 'How are you?'

milestone_celebration.title: First Words Unlocked! 🎉
milestone_celebration.description: Congratulations! You've learned your first 25 Arabic words and basic greetings. You can now introduce yourself and have simple conversations!
milestone_celebration.character_scene: Oustaz Abou Adam nods approvingly and says: 'Excellent! You've taken your first steps on this beautiful journey of learning Arabic. Onwards to Level 2!'
milestone_celebration.next_milestone: Level 2 Unlocked - Master the 28 Arabic Letters
